paragraphs.text: A RECENT earthquake at San Nicholas Island, near Santa Barbara, Cal., has revived interest in the story of the old Indian woman who led a Crusoe life on the island for many years. She was left there by accident in 1836, and her existence forgotton until twenty years later, when a Santa Barbara hunter, who had visited the island for otters, found her there and carried her to the mainland. She had entirely forgotten her language, and in consequence the story of her hermitage was never known. Three months after her rescue she died, and was buried by the mission fathers.
